title: Zuckerburg Admits AI Buildout Spend Has Not Been "Clean" - TCR 07/03/26
description:
Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg told staff AI agents haven't hit the schedule behind $125 billion in spending, 8,000 job cuts, and 7,000 reassignments, Nvidia began renting back unused GPUs for a cut of cloud revenue, and Berkeley startup Conception grew the first early human eggs from stem cells built out of a patient's own blood.

https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-july-3-2026/

0:00 Meta's AI-agent timeline meets reality
0:54 Nvidia backstops demand for its own chips
3:46 First early human eggs grown from stem cells
6:39 Factory-built nuclear reaches criticality
9:58 The Nuclear Supply Chain Moved All at Once
13:13 AI drug discovery earns a $660M validation
16:24 Synthesis and Closing

This episode covers Meta's AI-agent timeline admission and its still-in-training internal Watermelon model catching GPT-5.5 on key benchmarks, Nvidia's GPU backstop alongside the Meta and SoftBank neocloud units, Conception's stem-cell human eggs, three factory-built nuclear reactors reaching criticality plus Centrus's $900M HALEU contract, the Insilico-Takeda $660M deal and the COMPASS pan-cancer model, and the first UN scientific panel on AI.

The people funding this era are hedging on the timeline they raised money against. Zuckerberg said agent progress lagged Meta's 2026 plans even after $125 billion in commitments, while Alexandr Wang said an internal model still in training had pulled level with GPT-5.5 on key benchmarks, on an order of magnitude more compute. The bottleneck moved from reaching the frontier to trusting a frontier model to act on its own. Underneath, Nvidia started renting back its own idle GPUs for a share of revenue as Meta and SoftBank launched resale businesses, prying compute loose from single owners. The capability advanced at its own pace: Conception grew the first early human eggs from stem cells, three reactors reached criticality in one month alongside the first 3D-printed reactor core and a revived domestic HALEU line, a pan-cancer model read immunotherapy response across 33 tumor types, and Insilico drew roughly $660 million from Takeda. The first independent UN scientific panel on AI reported capability doubling in task complexity every four to seven months.
